scoring.must_pass: Hard runtime, artifact, action, reload, and truthful capability invariants. A failure blocks release.
scoring.should_pass: Quality or polish signal. A failure can ship only with an explicit follow-up owner.
scoring.watch: Exploratory behavior. Use for trends, not release gates.

scenarios.purpose: A normal user can ask a buy-and-hold benchmark question without needing template words.
natural_prompt_variants: i just wanna know if microsoft beat spy last year if i simply held it
show me how apple did if i had only held it for the past year
was nvidia better than the market over the last twelve months if i bought and held?

hard_checks: asset resolves to one equity symbol
period is last/past year when requested
no unnecessary asset or strategy clarification

artifact_checks: confirmation card has one canonical artifact id
ready-to-run appears only with validated launch payload
action_checks: run_backtest action is card-scoped when a confirmation card is shown
reload_checks: same card and card-scoped action hydrate after reload
forbidden_outcomes: asks which asset when prompt names an asset
scenarios.judge_rubric: Answer should be plain-language, specific to the named asset and period, and honest about assumptions.

scenarios.purpose: Argus keeps one strategy draft alive across natural edits instead of restarting the idea.
natural_prompt_variants: Test buying and holding Apple over the past year.
Backtest holding AAPL for a year.
Try a simple apple hold from the last twelve months.

variants: same draft but half a year
hard_checks: card updates only the date range

artifact_checks: single active confirmation lineage survives edits
action_checks: latest card action supersedes stale card actions
reload_checks: latest edited card hydrates, not the original card
forbidden_outcomes: cannot answer assumptions from the card
scenarios.judge_rubric: Continuity should feel like editing one object, not re-prompting a form.

scenarios.purpose: Ambiguous human dip language becomes a useful clarification and can be refined into executable RSI rules with user thresholds.
natural_prompt_variants: What if I bought Tesla after big drops?
test buying TSLA after it gets crushed
I want to buy Tesla dips

hard_checks: asks for condition clarification or offers executable dip proxies

variants: use RSI entry when it is 20 or lower and exit when it is 60 or higher over the last 3 months
make it RSI below 20, sell above 60, last quarter
use a technical indicator like RSI, buy at 20, exit at 60

artifact_checks: confirmation payload contains rule group for entry and exit
action_checks: run_backtest validates or explains data sufficiency
forbidden_outcomes: calls this a generic signal strategy without rule details
scenarios.judge_rubric: Answer should educate briefly while preserving the user's exact indicator choices.

scenarios.purpose: Vague momentum language maps to supported patterns or asks a useful clarifying question without brittle phrase matching.
natural_prompt_variants: Test buying SPY when it starts rising.
what if I buy strong stocks when momentum turns up
try momentum on Nvidia for the last month

hard_checks: asks for or proposes concrete supported momentum proxy
if choosing a default proxy, card names the exact rule
no false unsupported claim when a supported SMA/EMA crossover can represent the idea

artifact_checks: card must not hide generic Signal Strategy without rules
action_checks: adjust assumptions/change dates/change asset remain card-scoped
scenarios.judge_rubric: Argus should turn ambiguity into a concrete next move, not a dead end.

scenarios.purpose: Unsupported or incomplete compound indicator ideas preserve user intent and truthfully offer executable alternatives.
natural_prompt_variants: Test Nvidia when the 50-day moving average crosses above the 200-day moving average.
try MACD plus RSI on Apple, only buy if volume is above average
use Bollinger bands and RSI together for Tesla

hard_checks: names which parts are draft-only when not executable
offers executable alternatives without losing asset or period context
unsupported context is preserved as draft note

artifact_checks: draft-only intent is stored separately from executable payload
action_checks: run action appears only for executable subset
reload_checks: draft notes and executable subset hydrate
forbidden_outcomes: pretends unsupported compound rule can run
loses NVDA or chosen asset when simplifying
scenarios.judge_rubric: Capability truth should feel helpful, not apologetic or evasive.

scenarios.purpose: A visible Run backtest action executes from the visible artifact, not from fragile reinterpretation.
natural_prompt_variants: Backtest buy and hold Tesla over the past year.
Run a simple Tesla hold for last year.
test TSLA if I just held it over twelve months

hard_checks: ready card has validated launch payload
does not call interpretation model to rediscover strategy
creates result card or truthful executable failure

artifact_checks: artifact id and launch payload hash are present on action payload
action_checks: after reload, run_backtest still uses card payload
reload_checks: reload before running does not lose action context
forbidden_outcomes: visible run button fails because interpreter is unavailable
Argus says it lost context while the card is visible
stale card action executes a superseded card
scenarios.judge_rubric: User should feel the button is a real command, not a disguised prompt.

scenarios.purpose: Natural retry language resumes the latest failed action only as guidance, while quota-bearing runs remain button-triggered.
natural_prompt_variants: Can you try again?
try that again
No, run the same one.

hard_checks: failed action stores retryable payload and safe message
does not ask for asset again
does not auto-run quota-bearing backtest without card action

action_checks: retry guidance points to card action when execution is quota-bearing
reload_checks: latest failed action context survives reload
forbidden_outcomes: auto-runs because user said yes/run it outside card action
scenarios.judge_rubric: Retry should feel continuous while preserving explicit paid/quota action ownership.

scenarios.purpose: Completed runs become durable conversational facts for explanation, metrics, tested config, and next-step guidance.
natural_prompt_variants: Why did it underperform the benchmark?
What was the max drawdown?
What exactly did you test?
What should I try next?

hard_checks: recommendations are executable or clearly draft-only

artifact_checks: result card references immutable run id
action_checks: show_breakdown and save_strategy use latest run id
reload_checks: after reload, follow-up answers still use latest result
forbidden_outcomes: generic investing advice detached from run
scenarios.judge_rubric: Answer quality should combine fact grounding, concise interpretation, and safe next experiments.

scenarios.purpose: Result actions are stable, idempotent, and visibly tied to the completed run.
natural_prompt_variants: Show a breakdown
Save strategy
Refine strategy

hard_checks: asks for desired refinement or applies explicit refinement

artifact_checks: result action payloads carry run_id and conversation_id
action_checks: all visible result actions are clickable and have semantic ownership
reload_checks: saved and result context remain sane after reload
forbidden_outcomes: actions lose run context after reload
scenarios.judge_rubric: Actions should feel like operating the card, not sending vague follow-up prompts.

scenarios.purpose: Streaming, scrolling, controls, and copy feedback never make the user doubt whether the answer/action is complete.
natural_prompt_variants: Explain buying strong stocks in simple words.
why do people buy after a price drop?
what can I do here?

hard_checks: status text and assistant tokens do not compete
message controls are hidden until final

artifact_checks: no duplicate action chips near composer
action_checks: copy/feedback controls are scoped to final messages
forbidden_outcomes: scroll yanks user while reading older content
scenarios.judge_rubric: Polish should reduce cognitive doubt without adding clutter.

scenarios.purpose: Crypto ideas resolve through provider truth and same-asset validation without mixing incompatible universes.
natural_prompt_variants: Compare Ethereum and Bitcoin over the last year.
backtest holding ETH and BTC for a year
try Solana and Ethereum, last six months

hard_checks: benchmark is BTC unless pair/self rule applies

artifact_checks: asset class is explicit on launch payload
action_checks: run action appears only if provider/date data is sufficient
forbidden_outcomes: mixes equity and crypto in one run
asks for asset when prompt names crypto assets
scenarios.judge_rubric: Cross-asset constraints should be explained in product language.

scenarios.purpose: Spanish natural language uses the same artifact spine and mirrors the user's language.
natural_prompt_variants: Prueba comprar y mantener Nvidia durante el ultimo ano.
Quiero probar Apple si solo la mantenia seis meses.
Haz una prueba simple de Bitcoin del ultimo ano.

hard_checks: artifact shape is identical to English path

artifact_checks: confirmation/result card uses canonical metadata, not translated text parsing
forbidden_outcomes: routes through a separate Spanish hardcoded flow
falls back to English without user choice
scenarios.judge_rubric: Spanish should feel first-class while preserving product constraints.

scenarios.purpose: Capability answers come from the same provider/capability/compiler truth that execution uses.
natural_prompt_variants: What strategies do you support based on indicators?
Do you only support two indicators?
What are the limits of what I can do here?

hard_checks: lists only actually executable paths as executable
does not crash on broad capability questions

artifact_checks: no card unless the user asks to draft/test a concrete idea
action_checks: no quota-bearing run action appears for pure capability Q&A
reload_checks: answer remains a normal assistant message
forbidden_outcomes: I could not process that turn; try again in one sentence
narrows product to hardcoded strategy names
scenarios.judge_rubric: Capability explanation should be accurate, beginner-friendly, and invite a concrete next test.

scenarios.purpose: Assets resolve through provider-shaped catalogs and ambiguous names get useful clarification.
natural_prompt_variants: Test buying Meta last year.
what if I held Berkshire over the last two years?
try buying coinbase after drops

hard_checks: common company names resolve to active provider symbols when unambiguous
ambiguous names ask a focused clarification

action_checks: run action validates provider availability before display
forbidden_outcomes: asks which asset for an unambiguous provider match
scenarios.judge_rubric: Asset resolution should feel smart but not overconfident.
